subject: Human Resources
title: The History of Employee Engagement
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The History of Employee Engagement.

hook.text: In 1990, William Kahn published a paper asking a radical question: why do some workers bring their full selves to work?

buildup.text: Kahn's research identified three conditions for engagement: meaningfulness, safety, and availability.

discovery.text: Before Kahn, workplaces focused on compliance and productivity—not whether employees emotionally connected to their work.

twist.text: The concept was largely ignored until Gallup commercialized engagement surveys in the late 1990s—then it exploded.

climax.text: Today, engagement is a multi-billion dollar industry—consultants, platforms, and surveys—all from one academic paper.

punchline.text: One question changed everything: do people bring their full selves to work?

quiz.question: Who is credited with founding the concept of employee engagement?
